verdict.answer_first: B states the usable plan immediately: "verify → persist raw → ack fast → process async"; A first says "Good problem to get right" before the plan.
verdict.decisiveness: B commits cleanly to one pattern and keeps it linear; A also commits, but adds more framing and optional follow-up branches.
verdict.fabrication: B adds provider-specific examples not given: "HMAC-SHA256" and "±5 min"; A has broad claims but fewer invented specifics.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on because it is sequenced as endpoint, worker, backstop with concrete steps and less extra explanation.
